event: found down by a family member
admitted to the surgical intensive care unit
burr holes for evacuation of the SDH
strange sensation in the right arm
feeling that he could not control the arm
sent directly to the emergency room
transferred to the medical intensive care unit
discharged to long-term acute care hospital
localized with the right upper extremity
minimal movement of left upper and bilateral lower extremities
cough, gag, corneal, and pupillary reflexes were all present
prominence of the anterior temporal branch of the right middle cerebral artery
early, rapid, shunting of blood through a cortical vein to the superior sagittal sinus
opacification of a capillary-like serpiginous tangle of vessels
no opacification of the early draining vein or AVM nidus
AVM was separated from surrounding brain parenchyma
feeding arteries, followed by the draining vein, were coagulated
no arterial flow in the draining vein
associated intraparenchymal hematoma was also evacuated
spontaneous antigravity movement of the right upper extremity
no movement of the left upper or bilateral lower extremities
treated in consultation with infectious disease
